name: Apples in Gilded Cages
recipeIngredient: Strip of orange rind (about 2 inches long)
3 Candied cherries, cut in half (optional garnish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour 3 cups of water into an enameled or stainless-steel skillet.
2. Add the sugar, orange rind, and vanilla to the water. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat and let simmer. Meanwhile, peel apples, leaving stems intact and cutting a thin slice from the bottom for stability.
4. Add fruit to the syrup; it should reach halfway to two-thirds up the sides of the apples. If needed, add more water.
5. Cover skillet and gently poach apples until soft when pierced with a knife (about 20 minutes). Turn apples a few times during cooking.
6. Lift apples out of syrup with a slotted spoon and let drain and cool.
Sauce Preparation:
1. Measure 1 cup of cooking syrup into a small pot.
2. Mix 1 tablespoon water with 1 teaspoon cornstarch in a bowl, then stir into the syrup.
3. Add apple brandy and apple jelly to the mixture on medium heat. Cook until slightly thickened (light cream consistency).
4. Remove from heat and let cool.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F.
6. Roll out pastry dough to about 1/8-inch thickness.
7. Cut 6 circles using a 3- or 3-1/2-inch round cutter and place on a baking sheet.
8. Place a well-drained apple in each circle.
9. Cut remaining dough into 1/4-inch-wide strips, then cut into 3- to 4-inch lengths.
10. Place strips lengthwise from stem to bottom under the apples; press fruit onto them.
11. Wet edges with water and lightly press where strips touch.
12. If stems are long enough, wind pastry around them.
13. Roll out remaining dough again, shape into leaf forms, and place on top if desired.
14. Brush all dough with evaporated milk.
15. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until pastry turns dark golden brown.
